# Copilot Instructions

## GitHub Copilot Integration

This document describes how the LazyRegion project leverages GitHub Copilot to enhance developer productivity and code quality.

### Code Completion and Suggestions
GitHub Copilot provides AI-powered code completion and suggestions based on the context of the code you're writing. It can help you write code faster and with fewer errors by suggesting complete lines or entire functions.

When working on the LazyRegion project, you can use GitHub Copilot to:
- Generate boilerplate code for common patterns and scenarios
- Complete complex function implementations based on your intent
- Suggest best practices and idiomatic code for the project's languages and frameworks

To get the most out of GitHub Copilot, provide clear and descriptive comments or function names that express your intent. Copilot will use this context to generate more relevant and accurate suggestions.

### Architecture and Design Assistance
GitHub Copilot can also assist with architecture and design tasks by suggesting patterns, best practices, and examples based on the project's structure and conventions.

When working on the LazyRegion architecture, you can use GitHub Copilot to:
- Suggest appropriate design patterns and architectures based on the project's goals and constraints
- Generate sample code and implementations for new features or components
- Provide guidance on how to structure and organize code within the project's existing architecture

To leverage Copilot's architecture and design assistance, start by writing clear and concise documentation or comments that describe your goals, requirements, and constraints. Copilot will use this information to generate more relevant and helpful suggestions.

### Documentation and Code Examples
GitHub Copilot can help you write better documentation and code examples by suggesting content based on your comments and the project's context.

When documenting the LazyRegion project, you can use GitHub Copilot to:
- Generate code examples and snippets that illustrate how to use the project's components and APIs
- Suggest improvements to your documentation's structure, clarity, and completeness
- Provide sample code and explanations for common use cases and scenarios

To get the most out of Copilot's documentation assistance, start by writing clear and descriptive comments or placeholders that outline the structure and purpose of your documentation. Copilot will use this context to generate more relevant and helpful content.

### Best Practices and Usage Guidelines
To ensure a smooth and productive integration of GitHub Copilot into the LazyRegion project, follow these best practices and guidelines:
- Always review and test the code generated by Copilot before committing or merging it into the project
- Provide clear and descriptive comments, function names, and documentation to help Copilot generate more accurate and relevant suggestions
- Use Copilot as a tool to enhance your productivity, but not as a substitute for your own judgement and expertise
- Regularly update your local Copilot model to ensure you have access to the latest improvements and features

By following these guidelines and leveraging GitHub Copilot's capabilities, you can streamline your development process, write better code, and maintain a high level of quality and consistency throughout the LazyRegion project.

## 프로젝트 지침
- Prefer keeping `LazyRegionRegistry.OnManagerRequested` lazy initialization to allow `UseWpf` to be called in any order; also prefer providing `UseWpf(Action<LazyRegionApp>)` overload for safer configuration.
